response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack developer with strong software craftsmanship skills, but C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago. The role requires a current, hands-on C++ specialist with industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), MQTT/IoT, and message-broker experience — none of which appear in the resume. Recent work is centered on TypeScript/Node.js/DLang in web and data-platform domains, which does not align with the on-prem industrial automation focus.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software development experience, within the 8–12 year band
Demonstrated work on high-performance, low-latency, multi-threaded systems (Sociomantic) and event-driven architectures
Strong testing/TDD culture, mentoring, workshops, and technical communication track record
concerns: C++ exposure is minimal (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015) and stale; current stack is JS/TS/DLang
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA protocol experience
No demonstrated work with industrial message brokers, on-prem industrial platforms, or Azure cloud services

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Solid high-performance and distributed-systems engineering background
